description: A square, designed ICE 'arrest card' graphic built over a dramatic, desaturated black-and-white close-up photo of a pair of hands in metal handcuffs, shot at a low angle so the wrists and chain fill the frame. A dark gradient overlay across the left and bottom makes the white and pale-cyan text legible. Top-left, in a bold uppercase cyan serif headline: 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL,' with a white subhead '1/22/2025 | Denver, CO.' Lower left, in large bold white sans-serif, the detainee's name 'Pablo Beningo Calva-Deamonte,' followed by a 'CRIMES:' list reading '3 DUIs' and 'Unlawful reentering.' The official DHS/ICE seal and 'ICE' wordmark sit at the bottom left. A red diagonal banner across the bottom-right corner reads 'DENVER FIELD OFFICE.' The composition is the recurring ICE 'rogues-gallery' template: ominous monochrome enforcement imagery plus crisp branded typography, used to publicize an individual arrest and frame immigration enforcement as removal of criminal threats. Target audience: the public and supporters, to advertise enforcement results and deter unlawful reentry.
notable_text: ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL; 1/22/2025 | Denver, CO; Pablo Beningo Calva-Deamonte; CRIMES: 3 DUIs, Unlawful reentering; DENVER FIELD OFFICE
